---
title: "Software"
---

## Software tools to install

-   Current version of R
-   Current version of RStudio
-   A terminal that allows `ssh` (for cluster access, if applicable)
-   Git, and a GitHub account for version control

## R and RStudio

-   Current version of R ([install here](https://www.r-project.org))
-   Current version of RStudio ([install here](https://www.rstudio.org))

## R / Bioconductor packages commonly used for scRNA-seq

*Install the packages the workshop will use in a single setup session. Example:*

``` r
# CRAN
install.packages(c("tidyverse", "Seurat", "remotes"))

# Bioconductor
if (!require("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")
BiocManager::install(c(
  "SingleCellExperiment",
  "scran",
  "scater",
  "DropletUtils"
))
```

## Accessing the shell via a terminal application

### *Mac or Linux users*

-   Open the native "Terminal" app on Mac or Linux
-   Or install another terminal like "iTerm2"

### *Windows users*

-   Guide: <https://ubuntu.com/tutorials/install-ubuntu-on-wsl2-on-windows-10#1-overview>
-   Run Windows PowerShell as administrator
-   Install WSL2 by typing `wsl --install`
-   Restart your computer
-   Search for and install Ubuntu from the Microsoft Store
-   ***OR*** type `wsl --install -d ubuntu` on PowerShell to do both at once

### *Accessing the shell on Windows*

-   Open Ubuntu and set up a username and password (does not have to match your Windows login)
-   Run `sudo apt update` then `sudo apt upgrade` to ensure everything is up to date

## Git and GitHub

-   Current version of Git ([install here](https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Getting-Started-Installing-Git))
-   A GitHub account ([information here](https://github.com/signup))
